input.thought: Open official Ahrefs API and related official pages to verify auth, access, API shape, and rate limits, plus GitHub MCP evidence.

arguments.task: Review the official Ahrefs API page and nearby official documentation pages needed to verify: what the API is, authentication method, whether access requires a paid plan or contact sales, API surface type, rate limits, and any notable access restrictions. Return exact supporting text snippets with source URLs. Also navigate to official Ahrefs pricing/access pages if linked and capture relevant text.

Search GitHub for an Ahrefs MCP server or Model Context Protocol repository. Prefer official Ahrefs or clearly named community repositories. Return repository names, URLs, and whether an MCP server appears to exist.

raw_result.description: SEO data platform with a REST API for Ahrefs workspace data, keyword research, backlink analysis, and related integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Ahrefs API requires an API key; official docs say only workspace owners and admins can create and manage API keys in account settings.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public API docs are available, and API access is listed on self-serve paid Lite, Standard, and Advanced plans; Enterprise adds uncapped API access via sales, but API access is not partner-gated overall.

api_surface.breadth: Documented Ahrefs API v3 with OpenAPI-compatible endpoint reference covering Ahrefs workspace data and management/public areas; usage is constrained by API units and plan limits rather than lack of endpoint docs.
api_surface.mcp_notes: GitHub search shows an official archived repository at ahrefs/ahrefs-mcp-server, plus multiple community MCP-related repositories.

raw_result.blocker: Requires a paid Ahrefs plan and admin/owner ability to generate an API key; not self-serve free.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs state the API is limited to 60 requests per minute by default; exceeding it returns HTTP 429. Requests also consume API units, with a stated base cost of 50 units per request and dynamic throttling/unit-based limits documented separately. Free test queries and some endpoint groups do not consume units.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs explicitly describe the API as RESTful and OpenAPI-compatible. API keys are role-restricted to workspace owners/admins. Any paid plan can run limited free test queries, but those are restricted to specific Ahrefs-owned domains/keywords and capped at 100 results. Enterprise pricing page advertises uncapped API access via sales.
